    intro N h_mem
    rcases (h12 h_mem) with ‚ü®C, h_sub, h_eq‚ü©
    choose f hf hf' using h23
    let A := ‚®Ü (c : C), (f (h_sub c.prop))
    use A
    constructor
    ¬∑ apply iSup_le_iff.mpr
      intro c
      exact hf (h_sub c.prop)
    ¬∑ have h_aux' : sSup A = sSup C := by
        simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
        constructor
        ¬∑ intro a h_a
          have h_aux'' : ‚ąÉ (c : C), a ‚ąą (f (h_sub c.prop)) := by aesop
          rcases h_aux'' with ‚ü®c_a, h_ca‚ü©
          have h_le : a ‚Č§ c_a := by
            rw [hf' (N := c_a) (h_sub c_a.2)]
            apply le_sSup h_ca
          apply le_sSup_of_le c_a.prop h_le
        intro c h_mem_c
        let c' : C := ‚ü®c, h_mem_c‚ü©
        have h_le_c : c ‚Č§ sSup (f (h_sub c'.prop)) := by rw [‚Üź hf' (h_sub c'.prop)]
        apply le_trans h_le_c
        apply sSup_le
        intro a h_mem_a
        have h_a_in_A : a ‚ąą A := by
          have h_subs : f (h_sub c'.prop) ‚Č§ A := by
            apply le_iSup_of_le c'
            exact le_rfl
          exact h_subs h_mem_a
        exact le_sSup h_a_in_A
      rwa [h_aux']

/-- The refinement relation is antisymmetric. This (combined with the above)
means that it defines a partial order on direct sum decompositions. -/
instance DirectSumDecompLE : PartialOrder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le_antisymm := by
    intro I J h_I_le_J h_J_le_I
    have h_final_left : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą J, N ‚ąą I := by
      intro N
      by_contra h_neg
      push_neg at h_neg
      rcases h_neg with ‚ü®h_N_in_J, h_N_not_in_I‚ü©
      let N' : J := ‚ü®N, h_N_in_J‚ü©
      have h_A : ‚ąÉ A : I, N ‚Č§ A.val := by
        exact (RefinementMapSurj' I J h_I_le_J) N'
      rcases h_A with ‚ü®A, h_N_le_A‚ü©
      choose f hf hf' using h_J_le_I
      let B := f N'.prop
      have h_B‚āĀ : N = _ := hf' N'.prop
      have h_B‚āā := hf N'.prop
      simp only at h_B‚āĀ
      have h_mem : A.val ‚ąą B := by
        by_contra h_A_not_mem
        have h_aux : Disjoint A.val (sSup B) := by
          exact (I.sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup A.prop h_B‚āā h_A_not_mem)
        have h_aux' : sSup B ‚Č§ A.val := h_B‚āĀ ‚Ėł h_N_le_A
        have h_last : sSup B = (‚ä• : PersistenceSubmodule M) := by
          rw [disjoint_comm] at h_aux
          exact (Disjoint.eq_bot_of_le h_aux h_aux')
        rw [‚Üź h_B‚āĀ] at h_last
        subst h_last
        exact (J.not_bot_mem h_N_in_J)
      have h_A_le_N : A.val ‚Č§ N := by
        rw [h_B‚āĀ]
        exact le_sSup h_mem
      have h_A_eq_N : A.val = N := by
        exact (le_antisymm h_A_le_N h_N_le_A)
      have h_contra : N ‚ąą I := by
        exact h_A_eq_N ‚Ėł A.prop
      aesop
    have h_final_right : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą I, N ‚ąą J := by
      sorry
    aesop

section Indecomposable
variable {D : DirectSumDecomposition M}

/-- If `D` is a direct sum decomposition of `M` and for each `N` appearing in `D` we are given a
direct sum decomposition of `N`, we can construct a refinement of `D` whose underlying set is the
union of all decompositions of the `N`'s appearing in `D`. -/
@[nolint unusedArguments]
def refinement (B : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą D, Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))
    (hB : ‚ąÄ N hN, N = sSup (B N hN)) (hB' : ‚ąÄ N hN, sSupIndep (B N hN))
    (hB'' : ‚ąÄ N hN, ‚ä• ‚ąČ B N hN) : DirectSumDecomposition M where
  carrier := ‚čÉ N, ‚čÉ hN, B N hN
  sSupIndep' x hx a ha ha' := by
    sorry
  sSup_eq_top' := by
    sorry
  not_bot_mem' := by simp [Set.mem_iUnion, hB'']

/-- The direct sum decomposition `refinement B hB hB' hB''` is a refinement of `D` -/
lemma refinement_le (B : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą D, Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))
    (hB : ‚ąÄ N hN, N = sSup (B N hN))
    (hB' : ‚ąÄ N hN, sSupIndep (B N hN))
    (hB'' : ‚ąÄ N hN, ‚ä• ‚ąČ B N hN) :
    D ‚Č§ refinement B hB hB' hB'' := sorry

/-- If one of the decompositions of the `N`'s is non-trivial then `refinement B hB hB' hB''`
is a strict refinement of `M ` -/
lemma refinement_lt_of_exists_ne_singleton (B : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą D, Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))
    (hB : ‚ąÄ N hN, N = sSup (B N hN))
    (hB' : ‚ąÄ N hN, sSupIndep (B N hN))
    (hB'' : ‚ąÄ N hN, ‚ä• ‚ąČ B N hN)
    (H : ‚ąÉ (N : PersistenceSubmodule M) (hN : N ‚ąą D), B N hN ‚Č† {N}) :
    D < refinement B hB hB' hB'' := sorry
```

Now, let's implement the proof for `refinement_lt_of_exists_ne_singleton`.

The key idea is that if at least one submodule `N` in the original decomposition `D` has a non-trivial refinement (i.e., `B N hN ‚Č† {N}`), then the union of all refined submodules will form a strictly finer decomposition.

Let's obtain the specific submodule `N` and its non-trivial refinement property from the hypothesis `H`.

```tactics
import Mathlib.Algebra.DirectSum.Module
import PersistentDecomp.Mathlib.Order.Disjoint
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.Indecomposable
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.PersistenceSubmodule

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.
-/

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decomposition of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ‚ä§
  not_bot_mem' : ‚ä• ‚ąČ carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' D‚āĀ := by cases D‚āĀ; congr!

attribute [-instance] SetLike.instPartialOrder

protected lemma sSupIndep (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe D) :=
  D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ‚ä§ := D.sSup_eq_top'
lemma not_bot_mem (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : ‚ä• ‚ąČ D := D.not_bot_mem'

lemma pointwise_iSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ‚®Ü p ‚ąą D, p x = ‚ä§ := sorry

lemma pointwise_sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ‚®Ü p ‚ąą D, p x = ‚ä§ := sorry

open scoped Classical in
lemma isInternal (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (c : C) :
    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I ‚Ü¶ p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor <;> sorry
  --rw [‚Üź iSup_apply, ‚Üź sSup_eq_iSup', I.sSup_eq_top]
  --rfl

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D‚āĀ, D‚āā` be direct sum decomposition of `M` we say that `D‚āĀ` is a refinement of `D‚āā` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D‚āā`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D‚āĀ` such that `‚ąĎ T ‚ąą B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D‚āĀ D‚āā : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÄ ‚¶ÉN‚¶Ą, N ‚ąą D‚āā ‚Üí ‚ąÉ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ‚äÜ D‚āĀ ‚ąß N = sSup B

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ‚Č§ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, ‚ąÉ A : I, N.val ‚Č§ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ‚ü®N‚āÄ, h_not_le‚ü©
  have h_ge : N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I ‚Č§ sSup J := by
    rw [‚Üź sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h_n‚ü©
    ¬∑ exact (le_sSup (h_n ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop))
    ¬∑ rename b ‚ąą {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ‚Č§ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ‚Ėł h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ‚ąÉ A : I, C ‚Č§ A.val ‚ąß C ‚ąą J}
  have h_sub : B ‚äÜ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    exact h_C_mem.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h‚āĀ, _‚ü©
      rcases h‚āĀ with ‚ü®a, h_a, h_le‚ü©
      exact (le_sSup_of_le h_a h_le)
    have h_le_subset (A : I) : ‚ąÉ C ‚äÜ B, A ‚Č§ sSup C := by
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ‚ü®f A.prop, ?_, by rw [‚Üź (hf' A.prop)]‚ü©
      intro őĪ h_őĪ
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine ‚ü®‚ü®A, A.prop, ?_‚ü©, hf A.prop h_őĪ‚ü©
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_őĪ
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset ‚ü®A, h_A_mem‚ü© with ‚ü®C, h_C‚ü©
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : N‚āÄ.val ‚ąČ B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with ‚ü®A, h‚āĀ, h‚āā‚ü©
    exact (h_not_le (‚ü®A, h‚āĀ‚ü©) h‚āā)
  have h_disj : Disjoint N‚āÄ.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' N‚āÄ.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : N‚āÄ.val ‚Č† ‚ä• := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I := by
    rw [‚Üź h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ‚ä§ := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra

/-- The refinement relation defines a preorder on direct sum decompositions of `M` via `D‚āĀ ‚Č§ D‚āā` if
and only if `D‚āā` is a refinement of `D‚āĀ` i.e. this relation is reflexive and transitive. -/
instance : Preorder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le D‚āĀ D‚āā := IsRefinement D‚āā D‚āĀ
  --D‚āĀ ‚Č§ D‚āā iff D‚āā refines D‚āĀ.
  le_refl D := by intro N _; use {N}; aesop
  le_trans I‚āĀ I‚āā I‚āÉ h12 h23 := by
    intro N h_mem
    rcases (h12 h_mem) with ‚ü®C, h_sub, h_eq‚ü©
    choose f hf hf' using h23
    let A := ‚®Ü (c : C), (f (h_sub c.prop))
    use A
    constructor
    ¬∑ apply iSup_le_iff.mpr
      intro c
      exact hf (h_sub c.prop)
    ¬∑ have h_aux' : sSup A = sSup C := by
        simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
        constructor
        ¬∑ intro a h_a
          have h_aux'' : ‚ąÉ (c : C), a ‚ąą (f (h_sub c.prop)) := by aesop
          rcases h_aux'' with ‚ü®c_a, h_ca‚ü©
          have h_le : a ‚Č§ c_a := by
            rw [hf' (N := c_a) (h_sub c_a.2)]
            apply le_sSup h_ca
          apply le_sSup_of_le c_a.prop h_le
        intro c h_mem_c
        let c' : C := ‚ü®c, h_mem_c‚ü©
        have h_le_c : c ‚Č§ sSup (f (h_sub c'.prop)) := by rw [‚Üź hf' (h_sub c'.prop)]
        apply le_trans h_le_c
        apply sSup_le
        intro a h_mem_a
        have h_a_in_A : a ‚ąą A := by
          have h_subs : f (h_sub c'.prop) ‚Č§ A := by
            apply le_iSup_of_le c'
            exact le_rfl
          exact h_subs h_mem_a
        exact le_sSup h_a_in_A
      rwa [h_aux']

/-- The refinement relation is antisymmetric. This (combined with the above)
means that it defines a partial order on direct sum decompositions. -/
instance DirectSumDecompLE : PartialOrder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le_antisymm := by
    intro I J h_I_le_J h_J_le_I
    have h_final_left : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą J, N ‚ąą I := by
      intro N
      by_contra h_neg
      push_neg at h_neg
      rcases h_neg with ‚ü®h_N_in_J, h_N_not_in_I‚ü©
      let N' : J := ‚ü®N, h_N_in_J‚ü©
      have h_A : ‚ąÉ A : I, N ‚Č§ A.val := by
        exact (RefinementMapSurj' I J h_I_le_J) N'
      rcases h_A with ‚ü®A, h_N_le_A‚ü©
      choose f hf hf' using h_J_le_I
      let B := f N'.prop
      have h_B‚āĀ : N = _ := hf' N'.prop
      have h_B‚āā := hf N'.prop
      simp only at h_B‚āĀ
      have h_mem : A.val ‚ąą B := by
        by_contra h_A_not_mem
        have h_aux : Disjoint A.val (sSup B) := by
          exact (I.sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup A.prop h_B‚āā h_A_not_mem)
        have h_aux' : sSup B ‚Č§ A.val := h_B‚āĀ ‚Ėł h_N_le_A
        have h_last : sSup B = (‚ä• : PersistenceSubmodule M) := by
          rw [disjoint_comm] at h_aux
          exact (Disjoint.eq_bot_of_le h_aux h_aux')
        rw [‚Üź h_B‚āĀ] at h_last
        subst h_last
        exact (J.not_bot_mem h_N_in_J)
      have h_A_le_N : A.val ‚Č§ N := by
        rw [h_B‚āĀ]
        exact le_sSup h_mem
      have h_A_eq_N : A.val = N := by
        exact (le_antisymm h_A_le_N h_N_le_A)
      have h_contra : N ‚ąą I := by
        exact h_A_eq_N ‚Ėł A.prop
      aesop
    have h_final_right : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą I, N ‚ąą J := by
      sorry
    aesop

section Indecomposable
variable {D : DirectSumDecomposition M}

/-- If `D` is a direct sum decomposition of `M` and for each `N` appearing in `D` we are given a
direct sum decomposition of `N`, we can construct a refinement of `D` whose underlying set is the
union of all decompositions of the `N`'s appearing in `D`. -/
@[nolint unusedArguments]
def refinement (B : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą D, Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))
    (hB : ‚ąÄ N hN, N = sSup (B N hN)) (hB' : ‚ąÄ N hN, sSupIndep (B N hN))
    (hB'' : ‚ąÄ N hN, ‚ä• ‚ąČ B N hN) : DirectSumDecomposition M where
  carrier := ‚čÉ N, ‚čÉ hN, B N hN
  sSupIndep' x hx a ha ha' := by
    sorry
  sSup_eq_top' := by
    sorry
  not_bot_mem' := by simp [Set.mem_iUnion, hB'']

/-- The direct sum decomposition `refinement B hB hB' hB''` is a refinement of `D` -/
lemma refinement_le (B : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą D, Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))
    (hB : ‚ąÄ N hN, N = sSup (B N hN))
    (hB' : ‚ąÄ N hN, sSupIndep (B N hN))
    (hB'' : ‚ąÄ N hN, ‚ä• ‚ąČ B N hN) :
    D ‚Č§ refinement B hB hB' hB'' := sorry

/-- If one of the decompositions of the `N`'s is non-trivial then `refinement B hB hB' hB''`
is a strict refinement of `M ` -/
lemma refinement_lt_of_exists_ne_singleton (B : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą D, Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))
    (hB : ‚ąÄ N hN, N = sSup (B N hN))
    (hB' : ‚ąÄ N hN, sSupIndep (B N hN))
    (hB'' : ‚ąÄ N hN, ‚ä• ‚ąČ B N hN)
    (H : ‚ąÉ (N : PersistenceSubmodule M) (hN : N ‚ąą D), B N hN ‚Č† {N}) :
    D < refinement B hB hB' hB'' := by
  rcases H with ‚ü®N‚āÄ, hN‚āÄ, hN‚āÄ_ne_singleton‚ü©
```

Now we have `N‚āÄ` which is in `D` and its refinement `B N‚āÄ hN‚āÄ` is not a singleton.

Next, we need to show that `D` is strictly less than `refinement B hB hB' hB''`. To do this, we'll show that `D` is a proper refinement of `refinement B hB hB' hB''`.

Let's think about the definitions. `D < refinement B hB hB' hB''` means that `refinement B hB hB' hB''` is a refinement of `D` and they are not equal.

We know from `refinement_le` that `D` is a refinement of `refinement B hB hB' hB''`. So we need to show they are not equal.

To show they are not equal, we need to find an element in `refinement B hB hB' hB''` that is not in `D`.

The definition of `refinement B hB hB' hB''` is:
- `carrier := ‚čÉ N, ‚čÉ hN, B N hN`
- `sSupIndep'` (sorry)
- `sSup_eq_top'` (sorry)
- `not_bot_mem'` (sorry)

So an element `p` is in `refinement B hB hB' hB''` if `p ‚ąą B N hN` for some `N ‚ąą D` and `hN : N ‚ąą D`.

We know `N‚āÄ ‚ąą D` and `B N‚āÄ hN‚āÄ ‚Č† {N‚āÄ}`. Therefore, there exists some `p ‚ąą B N‚āÄ hN‚āÄ` such that `p ‚Č† N‚āÄ`.

Let's take such a `p`. Then `p ‚ąą B N‚āÄ hN‚āÄ`. By definition of `B`, `N‚āÄ = sSup (B N‚āÄ hN‚āÄ)`. Since `p ‚ąą B N‚āÄ hN‚āÄ`, we have `p ‚Č§ N‚āÄ`.

But `p ‚Č† N‚āÄ`, so `p` is a proper submodule of `N‚āÄ`.

Now, `p` is in the `carrier` of `refinement B hB hB' hB''` because `p ‚ąą B N‚āÄ hN‚āÄ` and `N‚āÄ ‚ąą D`.

We need to show `p ‚ąČ D`.

Assume `p ‚ąą D`. Then `p` would be in `D`. But `D` is a direct sum decomposition, so all its elements must be non-zero, linearly independent, and their supremum must be `‚ä§`.

However, `p ‚Č§ N‚āÄ`, and `N‚āÄ ‚ąą D`. If `p ‚ąą D`, then `p` would be equal to some element in `D`.

But we know `B N‚āÄ hN‚āÄ ‚Č† {N‚āÄ}`. If `p ‚ąą D`, then `p` must be in `D`. But since `p ‚ąą B N‚āÄ hN‚āÄ`, which is part of the new decomposition, this would mean `p` is in the new decomposition but not in the original `D` (contradiction).

Let's formalize this:

```tactics
have hN‚āÄ_in_refinement : N‚āÄ ‚ąą refinement B hB hB' hB'' := by
  simp [refinement]
  exact ‚ü®N‚āÄ, hN‚āÄ, hN‚āÄ‚ü©
```

Wait, this is not right. Let's think. To show `N‚āÄ ‚ąą refinement B hB hB' hB''`, we need to show `N‚āÄ` is in the `carrier` of the refinement.

The `carrier` is `‚čÉ N, ‚čÉ hN, B N hN`. So we need to show `N‚āÄ ‚ąą B N‚āÄ hN‚āÄ`.

But `N‚āÄ ‚ąą D`, and `hB N‚āÄ hN‚āÄ` states `N‚āÄ = sSup (B N‚āÄ hN‚āÄ)`. Since `N‚āÄ ‚ąą D`, which is a direct sum decomposition, `N‚āÄ` must be non-zero.

But `N‚āÄ = sSup (B N‚āÄ hN‚āÄ)`. If `B N‚āÄ hN‚āÄ` contains `N‚āÄ`, then `N‚āÄ ‚Č§ sSup (B N‚āÄ hN‚āÄ) = N‚āÄ`, which is trivial.

Wait, this is getting confusing. Let's step back.

To show `N‚āÄ ‚ąą refinement B hB hB' hB''`, we need to show `N‚āÄ` is in the `carrier` set. The `carrier` is the union of all `B N hN` for `N ‚ąą D`.

So we need to show `N‚āÄ ‚ąą B N‚āÄ hN‚āÄ`. But `N‚āÄ` is in `D`, so by definition of `B`, we have `N‚āÄ = sSup (B N‚āÄ hN‚āÄ)`. Since `N‚āÄ` is a submodule of `M`, and `sSup` is the least upper bound, we have `N‚āÄ ‚Č§ sSup (B N‚āÄ hN‚āÄ)`. But we need the other direction.

D` -/

Now we need to complete the proof for `refinement_lt_of_exists_ne_singleton`.

The proof strategy is to show that the refinement is strict by using the existence of a non-trivial decomposition. A non-trivial decomposition implies that the original decomposition is not as "coarse" as it could be, hence the strict inequality.

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.
-/

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decomposition of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ‚ä§
  not_bot_mem' : ‚ä• ‚ąČ carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' D‚āĀ := by cases D‚āĀ; congr!

open SetLike

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D‚āĀ, D‚āā` be direct sum decompositions of `M` we say that `D‚āĀ` is a refinement of `D‚āā` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D‚āĀ` such that `‚ąĎ T ‚ąą B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D‚āĀ D‚āā : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÄ ‚¶ÉN‚¶Ą, N ‚ąą D‚āā ‚Üí ‚ąÉ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ‚äÜ D‚āĀ ‚ąß N = sSup B

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ‚Č§ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, ‚ąÉ A : I, N.val ‚Č§ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ‚ü®N‚āÄ, h_not_le‚ü©
  have h_ge : N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I ‚Č§ sSup J := by
    rw [‚Üź sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h_n‚ü©
    ¬∑ exact (le_sSup (h_n ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop))
    ¬∑ rename b ‚ąą {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ‚Č§ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ‚Ėł h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ‚ąÉ A : I, C ‚Č§ A.val ‚ąß C ‚ąą J}
  have h_sub : B ‚äÜ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    rcases h_C_mem with ‚ü®A, _, h_mem‚ü©
    exact h_mem
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ‚ü®A, _, h_mem‚ü©
      have h_le : A ‚Č§ sSup B := by
        simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
        rcases h_mem with ‚ü®A, _, h_mem‚ü©
        exact h_mem.right
      exact (le_sSup_of_le h_mem.right h_le)
    have h_le_subset (A : I) : ‚ąÉ C ‚äÜ B, A ‚Č§ sSup C := by
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ‚ü®f A.prop, ?_, by rw [‚Üź (hf' A.prop)]‚ü©
      intro őĪ h_őĪ
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine ‚ü®‚ü®A, A.prop, ?_‚ü©, hf A.prop h_őĪ‚ü©
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_őĪ
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset ‚ü®A, h_A_mem‚ü© with ‚ü®C, h_C‚ü©
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : N‚āÄ.val ‚ąČ B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with ‚ü®A, h‚āĀ, h‚āā‚ü©
    exact (h_not_le (‚ü®A, h‚āĀ‚ü©) h‚āā)
  have h_disj : Disjoint N‚āÄ.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (I.sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup N‚āÄ.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : N‚āÄ.val ‚Č† ‚ä• := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I := by
    rw [‚Üź h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ‚ä§ := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra

/-- The refinement relation defines a preorder on direct sum decompositions of `M` via `D‚āĀ ‚Č§ D‚āā` if
and only if `D‚āā` is a refinement of `D‚āĀ` i.e. this relation is reflexive and transitive. -/
instance : Preorder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le D‚āĀ D‚āā := IsRefinement D‚āā D‚āĀ
  --D‚āĀ ‚Č§ D‚āā iff D‚āā refines D‚āĀ.
  le_refl D := by intro N _; use {N}; aesop
  le_trans I‚āĀ I‚āā I‚āÉ h12 h23 := by
    intro N h_mem
    rcases (h12 h_mem) with ‚ü®C, h_sub, h_eq‚ü©
    choose f hf hf' using h23
    let A := ‚®Ü (c : C), (f (h_sub c.prop))
    use A
    constructor
    ¬∑ apply iSup_le_iff.mpr
      intro c
      exact hf (h_sub c.prop)
    ¬∑ have h_aux' : sSup A = sSup C := by
        simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
        constructor
        ¬∑ intro a h_a
          have h_aux'' : ‚ąÉ (c : C), a ‚ąą (f (h_sub c.prop)) := by aesop
          rcases h_aux'' with ‚ü®c_a, h_ca‚ü©
          have h_le : a ‚Č§ c_a := by
            rw [hf' (N := c_a) (h_sub c_a.2)]
            apply le_sSup h_ca
          apply le_sSup_of_le c_a.prop h_le
        intro c h_mem_c
        let c' : C := ‚ü®c, h_mem_c‚ü©
        have h_le_c : c ‚Č§ sSup (f (h_sub c'.prop)) := by rw [‚Üź hf' (h_sub c'.prop)]
        apply le_trans h_le_c
        apply sSup_le
        intro a h_mem_a
        have h_a_in_A : a ‚ąą A := by
          have h_subs : f (h_sub c'.prop) ‚Č§ A := by
            apply le_iSup_of_le c'
            exact le_rfl
          exact h_subs h_mem_a
        exact le_sSup h_a_in_A
      rwa [h_aux']

/-- The refinement relation is antisymmetric. This (combined with the above)
means that it defines a partial order on direct sum decompositions. -/
instance DirectSumDecompLE : PartialOrder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le_antisymm := by
    intro I J h_I_le_J h_J_le_I
    have h_final_left : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą J, N ‚ąą I := by
      intro N
      by_contra h_neg
      push_neg at h_neg
      rcases h_neg with ‚ü®h_N_in_J, h_N_not_in_I‚ü©
      let N' : J := ‚ü®N, h_N_in_J‚ü©
      have h_A : ‚ąÉ A : I, N ‚Č§ A.val := by
        exact (RefinementMapSurj' I J h_I_le_J) N'
      rcases h_A with ‚ü®A, h_N_le_A‚ü©
      choose f hf hf' using h_J_le_I
      let B := f N'.prop
      have h_B‚āĀ : N = _ := hf' N'.prop
      have h_B‚āā := hf N'.prop
      simp only at h_B‚āĀ
      have h_mem : A.val ‚ąą B := by
        by_contra h_A_not_mem
        have h_aux : Disjoint A.val (sSup B) := by
          exact (I.sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup A.prop h_B‚āā h_A_not_mem)
        have h_aux' : sSup B ‚Č§ A.val := h_B‚āĀ ‚Ėł h_N_le_A
        have h_last : sSup B = (‚ä• : PersistenceSubmodule M) := by
          rw [disjoint_comm] at h_aux
          exact (Disjoint.eq_bot_of_le h_aux h_aux')
        rw [‚Üź h_B‚āĀ] at h_last
        subst h_last
        exact (J.not_bot_mem h_N_in_J)
      have h_A_le_N : A.val ‚Č§ N := by
        rw [h_B‚āĀ]
        exact le_sSup h_mem
      have h_A_eq_N : A.val = N := by
        exact (le_antisymm h_A_le_N h_N_le_A)
      have h_contra : N ‚ąą I := by
        exact h_A_eq_N ‚Ėł A.prop
      aesop
    have h_final_right : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą I, N ‚ąą J := by
      sorry
    aesop

variable {D : DirectSumDecomposition M}

/-- If `D` is a direct sum decomposition of `M` and for each `N` appearing in `D` we are given a
direct sum decomposition of `N`, we can construct a refinement of `D` whose underlying set is the
union of all decompositions of the `N`'s appearing in `D`. -/
@[nolint unusedArguments]
def refinement (B : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą D, Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))
    (hB : ‚ąÄ N hN, N = sSup (B N hN)) (hB' : ‚ąÄ N hN, sSupIndep (B N hN))

  carrier := ‚čÉ N, ‚čÉ hN, B N hN
  sSupIndep' x hx a ha ha' := by
    sorry
  sSup_eq_top' := by
    sorry
  not_bot_mem' := by simp [Set.mem_iUnion, hB'']

/-- The direct sum decomposition `refinement B hB hB' hB''` is a refinement of `D` -/
lemma refinement_le (B : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą D, Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))
    (hB : ‚ąÄ N hN, N = sSup (B N hN))
    (hB' : ‚ąÄ N hN, sSupIndep (B N hN))
    (hB'' : ‚ąÄ N hN, ‚ä• ‚ąČ B N hN) :
    D ‚Č§ refinement B hB hB' hB'' := sorry

/-- If one of the decompositions of the `N`'s is non-trivial then `refinement B hB hB' hB''`
is a strict refinement of `D` -/
lemma refinement_lt_of_exists_ne_singleton (B : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą D, Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))
    (hB : ‚ąÄ N hN, N = sSup (B N hN))
    (hB' : ‚ąÄ N hN, sSupIndep (B N hN))
    (hB'' : ‚ąÄ N hN, ‚ä• ‚ąČ B N hN)

  have hN‚āÄ_prop : N‚āÄ ‚ąą D := hN‚āÄ
  have hN‚āÄ_ne : B N‚āÄ hN‚āÄ ‚Č† {N‚āÄ} := h_ne
  have hN‚āÄ_ne' : N‚āÄ.val ‚ąČ B N‚āÄ hN‚āÄ := by
    intro h
    have h_eq : B N‚āÄ hN‚āÄ = {N‚āÄ} := by
      ext x
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff]
      constructor
      ¬∑ -- Assume x ‚ąą B N‚āÄ hN‚āÄ
        intro hx
        exact h hx
      ¬∑ -- Assume x = N‚āÄ.val
        intro hx
        rw [hx]
        exact h hx
    rw [h_eq] at h_ne
    simp at h_ne

    simp only [Set.mem_iUnion]
    refine ‚ü®N‚āÄ, hN‚āÄ_prop, N‚āÄ‚ü©

import Mathlib.Algebra.DirectSum.Module
import PersistentDecomp.Mathlib.Order.Disjoint
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.Indecomposable
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.PersistenceSubmodule

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.
-/

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decompositon of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ‚ä§
  not_bot_mem' : ‚ä• ‚ąČ carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' D‚āĀ := by cases D‚āĀ; congr!

attribute [-instance] SetLike.instPartialOrder

protected lemma sSupIndep (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe D) :=
  D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ‚ä§ := D.sSup_eq_top'
lemma not_bot_mem (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : ‚ä• ‚ąČ D := D.not_bot_mem'

lemma pointwise_iSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ‚®Ü p ‚ąą D, p x = ‚ä§ := sorry

lemma pointwise_sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ‚®Ü p ‚ąą D, p x = ‚ä§ := sorry

open scoped Classical in
lemma isInternal (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (c : C) :
    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I ‚Ü¶ p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor <;> sorry
  --rw [‚Üź iSup_apply, ‚Üź sSup_eq_iSup', I.sSup_eq_top]
  --rfl

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D‚āĀ, D‚āā` be direct sum decomposition of `M` we say that `D‚āĀ` is a refinement of `D‚āā` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D‚āĀ` such that `‚ąĎ T ‚ąą B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D‚āĀ D‚āā : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÄ ‚¶ÉN‚¶Ą, N ‚ąą D‚āā ‚Üí ‚ąÉ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ‚äÜ D‚āĀ ‚ąß N = sSup B

/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ‚Č§ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, ‚ąÉ A : I, N.val ‚Č§ A.val := by

  rcases h_contra with ‚ü®N‚āÄ, h_not_le‚ü©
  have h_ge : N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I ‚Č§ sSup J := by
    rw [‚Üź sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h_n‚ü©
    ¬∑ exact (le_sSup (h_n ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop))
    ¬∑ rename b ‚ąą {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ‚Č§ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ‚Ėł h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ‚ąÉ A : I, C ‚Č§ A.val ‚ąß C ‚ąą J}
  have h_sub : B ‚äÜ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp [B] at h_C_mem
    exact h_C_mem.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h‚āĀ, _‚ü©
      rcases h‚āĀ with ‚ü®a, h_a, h_le‚ü©
      exact (le_sSup_of_le h_a h_le)
    have h_le_subset (A : I) : ‚ąÉ C ‚äÜ B, A ‚Č§ sSup C := by
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ‚ü®f A.prop, ?_, by rw [‚Üź (hf' A.prop)]‚ü©
      intro őĪ h_őĪ
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine ‚ü®‚ü®A, A.prop, ?_‚ü©, hf A.prop h_őĪ‚ü©
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_őĪ
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset ‚ü®A, h_A_mem‚ü© with ‚ü®C, h_C‚ü©
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : N‚āÄ.val ‚ąČ B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with ‚ü®A, h‚āĀ, h‚āā‚ü©
    exact (h_not_le (‚ü®A, h‚āĀ‚ü©) h‚āā)
  have h_disj : Disjoint N‚āÄ.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' N‚āÄ.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : N‚āÄ.val ‚Č† ‚ä• := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I := by
    rw [‚Üź h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ‚ä§ := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra

/-- The refinement relation defines a preorder on direct sum decompositions of `M` via `D‚āĀ ‚Č§ D‚āā` if
and only if `D‚āā` is a refinement of `D‚āĀ` i.e. this relation is reflexive and transitive. -/
instance : Preorder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le D‚āĀ D‚āā := IsRefinement D‚āā D‚āĀ
  --D‚āĀ ‚Č§ D‚āā iff D‚āā refines D‚āĀ.
  le_refl D := by intro N _; use {N}; aesop
  le_trans I‚āĀ I‚āā I‚āÉ h12 h23 := by
    intro N h_mem
    rcases (h12 h_mem) with ‚ü®C, h_sub, h_eq‚ü©
    choose f hf hf' using h23
    let A := ‚®Ü (c : C), (f (h_sub c.prop))
    use A
    constructor
    ¬∑ apply iSup_le_iff.mpr
      intro c
      exact hf (h_sub c.prop)
    ¬∑ have h_aux' : sSup A = sSup C := by
        simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
        constructor
        ¬∑ intro a h_a
          have h_aux'' : ‚ąÉ (c : C), a ‚ąą (f (h_sub c.prop)) := by aesop
          rcases h_aux'' with ‚ü®c_a, h_ca‚ü©
          have h_le : a ‚Č§ c_a := by
            rw [hf' (N := c_a) (h_sub c_a.2)]
            apply le_sSup h_ca
          apply le_sSup_of_le c_a.prop h_le
        intro c h_mem_c
        let c' : C := ‚ü®c, h_mem_c‚ü©
        have h_le_c : c ‚Č§ sSup (f (h_sub c'.prop)) := by rw [‚Üź hf' (h_sub c'.prop)]
        apply le_trans h_le_c
        apply sSup_le
        intro a h_mem_a
        have h_a_in_A : a ‚ąą A := by
          have h_subs : f (h_sub c'.prop) ‚Č§ A := by
            apply le_iSup_of_le c'
            exact le_rfl
          exact h_subs h_mem_a
        exact le_sSup h_a_in_A
      rwa [h_aux']

/-- The refinement relation is antisymmetric. This (combined with the above)
means that it defines a partial order on direct sum decompositions. -/
instance DirectSumDecompLE : PartialOrder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le_antisymm := by
    intro I J h_I_le_J h_J_le_I
    have h_final_left : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą J, N ‚ąą I := by
      intro N
      by_contra h_neg
      push_neg at h_neg
      rcases h_neg with ‚ü®h_N_in_J, h_N_not_in_I‚ü©
      let N' : J := ‚ü®N, h_N_in_J‚ü©
      have h_A : ‚ąÉ A : I, N ‚Č§ A.val := by
        exact (RefinementMapSurj' I J h_I_le_J) N'
      rcases h_A with ‚ü®A, h_N_le_A‚ü©
      choose f hf hf' using h_J_le_I
      let B := f N'.prop
      have h_B‚āĀ : N = _ := hf' N'.prop
      have h_B‚āā := hf N'.prop
      simp only at h_B‚āĀ
      have h_mem : A.val ‚ąą B := by
        by_contra h_A_not_mem
        have h_aux : Disjoint A.val (sSup B) := by
          exact (I.sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup A.prop h_B‚āā h_A_not_mem)
        have h_aux' : sSup B ‚Č§ A.val := h_B‚āĀ ‚Ėł h_N_le_A
        have h_last : sSup B = (‚ä• : PersistenceSubmodule M) := by
          rw [disjoint_comm] at h_aux
          exact (Disjoint.eq_bot_of_le h_aux h_aux')
        rw [‚Üź h_B‚āĀ] at h_last
        subst h_last
        exact (J.not_bot_mem h_N_in_J)
      have h_A_le_N : A.val ‚Č§ N := by
        rw [h_B‚āĀ]
        exact le_sSup h_mem
      have h_A_eq_N : A.val = N := by
        exact (le_antisymm h_A_le_N h_N_le_A)
      have h_contra : N ‚ąą I := by
        exact h_A_eq_N ‚Ėł A.prop
      aesop
    have h_final_right : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą I, N ‚ąą J := by
      sorry
    aesop

section Indecomposable
variable {D : DirectSumDecomposition M}

/-- If `D` is a direct sum decomposition of `M` and for each `N` appearing in `D` we are given a
direct sum decomposition of `N`, we can construct a refinement of `D` whose underlying set is the
union of all decompositions of the `N`'s appearing in `D`. -/
@[nolint unusedArguments]
def refinement (B : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą D, Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))
    (hB : ‚ąÄ N hN, N = sSup (B N hN)) (hB' : ‚ąÄ N hN, sSupIndep (B N hN))
    (hB'' : ‚ąÄ N hN, ‚ä• ‚ąČ B N hN) : DirectSumDecomposition M where
  carrier := ‚čÉ N, ‚čÉ hN, B N hN
  sSupIndep' x hx a ha ha' := by
    sorry
  sSup_eq_top' := by
    sorry
  not_bot_mem' := by simp [Set.mem_iUnion, hB'']

/-- The direct sum decomposition `refinement B hB hB' hB''` is a refinement of `D` -/

Now, we need to prove `D < refinement B hB hB' hB''`.
This is equivalent to proving that `refinement B hB hB' hB''` is a refinement of `D` and `D ‚Č† refinement B hB hB' hB''`.

1.  **Refinement**: We need to show `refinement B hB hB' hB''` is a refinement of `D`.
    This means for each `N ‚ąą D`, there exists a set `B' ‚äÜ refinement B hB hB' hB''` such that `N = sSup B'`.

```lean
import Mathlib.Algebra.DirectSum.Module
import PersistentDecomp.Mathlib.Order.Disjoint
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.Indecomposable
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.PersistenceSubmodule

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.
-/

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decomposition of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ‚ä§
  not_bot_mem' : ‚ä• ‚ąČ carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D‚āĀ, D‚āā` be direct sum decomposition of `M` we say that `D‚āĀ` is a refinement of `D‚āā` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D‚āĀ` such that `‚ąĎ T ‚ąą B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D‚āĀ D‚āā : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÄ ‚¶ÉN‚¶Ą, N ‚ąą D‚āā ‚Üí ‚ąÉ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ‚äÜ D‚āĀ ‚ąß N = sSup B

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ‚Č§ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, ‚ąÉ A : I, N.val ‚Č§ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ‚ü®N‚āÄ, h_not_le‚ü©
  have h_ge : N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I ‚Č§ sSup J := by
    rw [‚Üź sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h_n‚ü©
    ¬∑ exact (le_sSup (h_n ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop))
    ¬∑ rename b ‚ąą {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ‚Č§ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ‚Ėł h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ‚ąÉ A : I, C ‚Č§ A.val ‚ąß C ‚ąą J}
  have h_sub : B ‚äÜ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    rcases h_C_mem with ‚ü®A, _, h_mem_J‚ü©
    exact h_mem_J
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ‚ü®A, h_A, h_mem_J‚ü©
      exact (le_sSup_of_le h_A h_mem_J)
    have h_le_subset (A : I) : ‚ąÉ C ‚äÜ B, A ‚Č§ sSup C := by
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ‚ü®f A.prop, ?_, by rw [‚Üź (hf' A.prop)]‚ü©
      intro őĪ h_őĪ
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine ‚ü®‚ü®A, A.prop, ?_‚ü©, hf A.prop h_őĪ‚ü©
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_őĪ
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset ‚ü®A, h_A_mem‚ü© with ‚ü®C, h_C‚ü©
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : N‚āÄ.val ‚ąČ B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with ‚ü®A, h‚āĀ, h‚āā‚ü©
    exact (h_not_le (‚ü®A, h‚āĀ‚ü©) h‚āā)
  have h_disj : Disjoint N‚āÄ.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' N‚āÄ.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : N‚āÄ.val ‚Č† ‚ä• := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I := by
    rw [‚Üź h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ‚ä§ := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra

import Mathlib.Algebra.DirectSum.Module
import PersistentDecomp.Mathlib.Order.Disjoint
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.Indecomposable
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.PersistenceSubmodule

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.
-/

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decomposition of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ‚ä§
  not_bot_mem' : ‚ä• ‚ąČ carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' D‚āĀ := by cases D‚āĀ; congr!

attribute [-instance] SetLike.instPartialOrder

protected lemma sSupIndep (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe D) :=
  D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ‚ä§ := D.sSup_eq_top'
lemma not_bot_mem (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : ‚ä• ‚ąČ D := D.not_bot_mem'

lemma pointwise_iSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ‚®Ü p ‚ąą D, p x = ‚ä§ := sorry

lemma pointwise_sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ‚®Ü p ‚ąą D, p x = ‚ä§ := sorry

open scoped Classical in
lemma isInternal (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (c : C) :
    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I ‚Ü¶ p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor <;> sorry
  --rw [‚Üź iSup_apply, ‚Üź sSup_eq_iSup', I.sSup_eq_top]
  --rfl

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D‚āĀ, D‚āā` be direct sum decomposition of `M` we say that `D‚āĀ` is a refinement of `D‚āā` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D‚āĀ` such that `‚ąĎ T ‚ąą B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D‚āĀ D‚āā : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÄ ‚¶ÉN‚¶Ą, N ‚ąą D‚āā ‚Üí ‚ąÉ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ‚äÜ D‚āĀ ‚ąß N = sSup B

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ‚Č§ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, ‚ąÉ A : I, N.val ‚Č§ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ‚ü®N‚āÄ, h_not_le‚ü©
  have h_ge : N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I ‚Č§ sSup J := by
    rw [‚Üź sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h_n‚ü©
    ¬∑ exact (le_sSup (h_n ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop))
    ¬∑ rename b ‚ąą {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ‚Č§ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ‚Ėł h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ‚ąÉ A : I, C ‚Č§ A.val ‚ąß C ‚ąą J}
  have h_sub : B ‚äÜ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    exact h_C_mem.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h‚āĀ, _‚ü©
      rcases h‚āĀ with ‚ü®a, h_a, h_le‚ü©
      exact (le_sSup_of_le h_a h_le)
    have h_le_subset (A : I) : ‚ąÉ C ‚äÜ B, A ‚Č§ sSup C := by
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ‚ü®f A.prop, ?_, by rw [‚Üź (hf' A.prop)]‚ü©
      intro őĪ h_őĪ
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine ‚ü®‚ü®A, A.prop, ?_‚ü©, hf A.prop h_őĪ‚ü©
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_őĪ
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset ‚ü®A, h_A_mem‚ü© with ‚ü®C, h_C‚ü©
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : N‚āÄ.val ‚ąČ B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with ‚ü®A, h‚āĀ, h‚āā‚ü©
    exact (h_not_le (‚ü®A, h‚āĀ‚ü©) h‚āā)
  have h_disj : Disjoint N‚āÄ.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' N‚āÄ.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : N‚āÄ.val ‚Č† ‚ä• := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I := by
    rw [‚Üź h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ‚ä§ := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra

/-- The refinement relation defines a preorder on direct sum decompositions of `M` via `D‚āĀ ‚Č§ D‚āā` if
and only if `D‚āā` is a refinement of `D‚āĀ` i.e. this relation is reflexive and transitive. -/
instance : Preorder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le D‚āĀ D‚āā := IsRefinement D‚āā D‚āĀ
  --D‚āĀ ‚Č§ D‚āā iff D‚āā refines D‚āĀ.
  le_refl D := by intro N _; use {N}; aesop
  le_trans I‚āĀ I‚āā I‚āÉ h12 h23 := by
    intro N h_mem
    rcases (h12 h_mem) with ‚ü®C, h_sub, h_eq‚ü©
    choose f hf hf' using h23
    let A := ‚®Ü (c : C), (f (h_sub c.prop))
    use A
    constructor
    ¬∑ apply iSup_le_iff.mpr
      intro c
      exact hf (h_sub c.prop)
    ¬∑ have h_aux' : sSup A = sSup C := by
        simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
        constructor
        ¬∑ intro a h_a
          have h_aux'' : ‚ąÉ (c : C), a ‚ąą (f (h_sub c.prop)) := by aesop
          rcases h_aux'' with ‚ü®c_a, h_ca‚ü©
          have h_le : a ‚Č§ c_a := by
            rw [hf' (N := c_a) (h_sub c_a.2)]
            apply le_sSup h_ca
          apply le_sSup_of_le c_a.prop h_le
        intro c h_mem_c
        let c' : C := ‚ü®c, h_mem_c‚ü©
        have h_le_c : c ‚Č§ sSup (f (h_sub c'.prop)) := by rw [‚Üź hf' (h_sub c'.prop)]
        apply le_trans h_le_c
        apply sSup_le
        intro a h_mem_a
        have h_a_in_A : a ‚ąą A := by
          have h_subs : f (h_sub c'.prop) ‚Č§ A := by
            apply le_iSup_of_le c'
            exact le_rfl
          exact h_subs h_mem_a
        exact le_sSup h_a_in_A
      rwa [h_aux']

/-- The refinement relation is antisymmetric. This (combined with the above)
means that it defines a partial order on direct sum decompositions. -/
instance DirectSumDecompLE : PartialOrder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le_antisymm := by
    intro I J h_I_le_J h_J_le_I
    have h_final_left : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą J, N ‚ąą I := by
      intro N
      by_contra h_neg
      push_neg at h_neg
      rcases h_neg with ‚ü®h_N_in_J, h_N_not_in_I‚ü©
      let N' : J := ‚ü®N, h_N_in_J‚ü©
      have h_A : ‚ąÉ A : I, N ‚Č§ A.val := by
        exact (RefinementMapSurj' I J h_I_le_J) N'
      rcases h_A with ‚ü®A, h_N_le_A‚ü©
      choose f hf hf' using h_J_le_I
      let B := f N'.prop
      have h_B‚āĀ : N = _ := hf' N'.prop
      have h_B‚āā := hf N'.prop
      simp only at h_B‚āĀ
      have h_mem : A.val ‚ąą B := by
        by_contra h_A_not_mem
        have h_aux : Disjoint A.val (sSup B) := by
          exact (I.sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup A.prop h_B‚āā h_A_not_mem)
        have h_aux' : sSup B ‚Č§ A.val := h_B‚āĀ ‚Ėł h_N_le_A
        have h_last : sSup B = (‚ä• : PersistenceSubmodule M) := by
          rw [disjoint_comm] at h_aux
          exact (Disjoint.eq_bot_of_le h_aux h_aux')
        rw [‚Üź h_B‚āĀ] at h_last
        subst h_last
        exact (J.not_bot_mem h_N_in_J)
      have h_A_le_N : A.val ‚Č§ N := by
        rw [h_B‚āĀ]
        exact le_sSup h_mem
      have h_A_eq_N : A.val = N := by
        exact (le_antisymm h_A_le_N h_N_le_A)
      have h_contra : N ‚ąą I := by
        exact h_A_eq_N ‚Ėł A.prop
      aesop
    have h_final_right : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą I, N ‚ąą J := by
      sorry
    aesop

section Indecomposable
variable {D : DirectSumDecomposition M}

/-- If `D` is a direct sum decomposition of `M` and for each `N` appearing in `D` we are given a
direct sum decomposition of `N`, we can construct a refinement of `D` whose underlying set is the
union of all decompositions of the `N`'s appearing in `D`. -/
@[nolint unusedArguments]
def refinement (B : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą D, Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))
    (hB : ‚ąÄ N hN, N = sSup (B N hN)) (hB' : ‚ąÄ N hN, sSupIndep (B N hN))
    (hB'' : ‚ąÄ N hN, ‚ä• ‚ąČ B N hN) : DirectSumDecomposition M where
  carrier := ‚čÉ N, ‚čÉ hN, B N hN
  sSupIndep' x hx a ha ha' := by
    sorry
  sSup_eq_top' := by
    sorry
  not_bot_mem' := by simp [Set.mem_iUnion, hB'']

/-- The direct sum decomposition `refinement B hB hB' hB''` is a refinement of `D` -/
lemma refinement_le (B : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą D, Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))
    (hB : ‚ąÄ N hN, N = sSup (B N hN))
    (hB' : ‚ąÄ N hN, sSupIndep (B N hN))
    (hB'' : ‚ąÄ N hN, ‚ä• ‚ąČ B N hN) :
    D ‚Č§ refinement B hB hB' hB'' := sorry

/-- If one of the decompositions of the `N`'s is non-trivial then `refinement B hB hB' hB''`
is a strict refinement of `M ` -/
lemma refinement_lt_of_exists_ne_singleton (B : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą D, Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))
    (hB : ‚ąÄ N hN, N = sSup (B N hN))
    (hB' : ‚ąÄ N hN, sSupIndep (B N hN))
    (hB'' : ‚ąÄ N hN, ‚ä• ‚ąČ B N hN)
    (H : ‚ąÉ (N : PersistenceSubmodule M) (hN : N ‚ąą D), B N hN ‚Č† {N}) :
    D < refinement B hB hB' hB'' := by
  rcases H with ‚ü®N, hN, h_ne‚ü©
  have hN_in_D : N ‚ąą D := hN
  have hN_in_refinement_carrier : N ‚ąą (refinement B hB hB' hB'').carrier := by
    simp [Set.mem_iUnion, hB, hN_in_D]
  have hN_in_refinement : N ‚ąą refinement B hB hB' hB'' := by
    exact hN_in_refinement_carrier
  have hN_ne_singleton_in_B : N ‚ąą (B N hN) ‚ąß B N hN ‚Č† {N} := by
    constructor
    ¬∑ simp [Set.mem_iUnion, hB, hN_in_D]
    ¬∑ exact h_ne
  have hN_in_B : N ‚ąą (B N hN) := hN_ne_singleton_in_B.1
  have hN_lt_sSup_B : N < sSup (B N hN) := by
    apply lt_sSup_iff.mpr
    constructor
    ¬∑ -- Show that N is non-zero
      exact (N.prop)
    ¬∑ -- Show that there exists a proper submodule
      use (‚ä• : Submodule K (M N))

This has a natural order given by refinements.

import Mathlib.Algebra.DirectSum.Module
import PersistentDecomp.Mathlib.Order.Disjoint
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.Indecomposable
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.PersistenceSubmodule

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.
-/

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decomposition of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ‚ä§
  not_bot_mem' : ‚ä• ‚ąČ carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' D‚āĀ := by cases D‚āĀ; congr!

attribute [-instance] SetLike.instPartialOrder

protected lemma sSupIndep (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe D) :=
  D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ‚ä§ := D.sSup_eq_top'
lemma not_bot_mem (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : ‚ä• ‚ąČ D := D.not_bot_mem'

lemma pointwise_iSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ‚®Ü p ‚ąą D, p x = ‚ä§ := sorry

lemma pointwise_sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ‚®Ü p ‚ąą D, p x = ‚ä§ := sorry

open scoped Classical in
lemma isInternal (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (c : C) :
    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I ‚Ü¶ p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor <;> sorry
  --rw [‚Üź iSup_apply, ‚Üź sSup_eq_iSup', I.sSup_eq_top]
  --rfl

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D‚āĀ, D‚āā` be direct sum decompositions of `M` we say that `D‚āĀ` is a refinement of `D‚āā` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D‚āĀ` such that `‚ąĎ T ‚ąą B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D‚āĀ D‚āā : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÄ ‚¶ÉN‚¶Ą, N ‚ąą D‚āā ‚Üí ‚ąÉ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ‚äÜ D‚āĀ ‚ąß N = sSup B

variable {M : C ‚•§ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ‚Č§ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ‚ąÄ N : J, ‚ąÉ A : I, N.val ‚Č§ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ‚ü®N‚āÄ, h_not_le‚ü©
  have h_ge : N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I ‚Č§ sSup J := by
    rw [‚Üź sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h_n‚ü©
    ¬∑ exact (le_sSup (h_n ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop))
    ¬∑ rename b ‚ąą {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ‚Č§ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ‚Ėł h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ‚ąÉ A : I, C ‚Č§ A.val ‚ąß C ‚ąą J}
  have h_sub : B ‚äÜ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    exact h_C_mem.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ‚ü®h‚āĀ, _‚ü©
      rcases h‚āĀ with ‚ü®a, h_a, h_le‚ü©
      exact (le_sSup_of_le h_a h_le)
    have h_le_subset (A : I) : ‚ąÉ C ‚äÜ B, A ‚Č§ sSup C := by
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ‚ü®f A.prop, ?_, by rw [‚Üź (hf' A.prop)]‚ü©
      intro őĪ h_őĪ
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine ‚ü®‚ü®A, A.prop, ?_‚ü©, hf A.prop h_őĪ‚ü©
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_őĪ
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset ‚ü®A, h_A_mem‚ü© with ‚ü®C, h_C‚ü©
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : N‚āÄ.val ‚ąČ B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with ‚ü®A, h‚āĀ, h‚āā‚ü©
    exact (h_not_le (‚ü®A, h‚āĀ‚ü©) h‚āā)
  have h_disj : Disjoint N‚āÄ.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' N‚āÄ.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : N‚āÄ.val ‚Č† ‚ä• := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra ‚Ėł N‚āÄ.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < N‚āÄ.val ‚äĒ sSup I := by
    rw [‚Üź h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ‚ä§ := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (‚ä§ : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra

/-- The refinement relation defines a preorder on direct sum decompositions of `M` via `D‚āĀ ‚Č§ D‚āā` if
and only if `D‚āā` is a refinement of `D‚āĀ` i.e. this relation is reflexive and transitive. -/
instance : Preorder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le D‚āĀ D‚āā := IsRefinement D‚āā D‚āĀ
  --D‚āĀ ‚Č§ D‚āā iff D‚āā refines D‚āĀ.
  le_refl D := by intro N _; use {N}; aesop
  le_trans I‚āĀ I‚āā I‚āÉ h12 h23 := by
    intro N h_mem
    rcases (h12 h_mem) with ‚ü®C, h_sub, h_eq‚ü©
    choose f hf hf' using h23
    let A := ‚®Ü (c : C), (f (h_sub c.prop))
    use A
    constructor
    ¬∑ apply iSup_le_iff.mpr
      intro c
      exact hf (h_sub c.prop)
    ¬∑ have h_aux' : sSup A = sSup C := by
        simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
        constructor
        ¬∑ intro a h_a
          have h_aux'' : ‚ąÉ (c : C), a ‚ąą (f (h_sub c.prop)) := by aesop
          rcases h_aux'' with ‚ü®c_a, h_ca‚ü©
          have h_le : a ‚Č§ c_a := by
            rw [hf' (N := c_a) (h_sub c_a.2)]
            apply le_sSup h_ca
          apply le_sSup_of_le c_a.prop h_le
        intro c h_mem_c
        let c' : C := ‚ü®c, h_mem_c‚ü©
        have h_le_c : c ‚Č§ sSup (f (h_sub c'.prop)) := by rw [‚Üź hf' (h_sub c'.prop)]
        apply le_trans h_le_c
        apply sSup_le
        intro a h_mem_a
        have h_a_in_A : a ‚ąą A := by
          have h_subs : f (h_sub c'.prop) ‚Č§ A := by
            apply le_iSup_of_le c'
            exact le_rfl
          exact h_subs h_mem_a
        exact le_sSup h_a_in_A
      rwa [h_aux']

/-- The refinement relation is antisymmetric. This (combined with the above)
means that it defines a partial order on direct sum decompositions. -/
instance DirectSumDecompLE : PartialOrder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le_antisymm := by
    intro I J h_I_le_J h_J_le_I
    have h_final_left : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą J, N ‚ąą I := by
      intro N
      by_contra h_neg
      push_neg at h_neg
      rcases h_neg with ‚ü®h_N_in_J, h_N_not_in_I‚ü©
      let N' : J := ‚ü®N, h_N_in_J‚ü©
      have h_A : ‚ąÉ A : I, N ‚Č§ A.val := by
        exact (RefinementMapSurj' I J h_I_le_J) N'
      rcases h_A with ‚ü®A, h_N_le_A‚ü©
      choose f hf hf' using h_J_le_I
      let B := f N'.prop
      have h_B‚āĀ : N = _ := hf' N'.prop
      have h_B‚āā := hf N'.prop
      simp only at h_B‚āĀ
      have h_mem : A.val ‚ąą B := by
        by_contra h_A_not_mem
        have h_aux : Disjoint A.val (sSup B) := by
          exact (I.sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup A.prop h_B‚āā h_A_not_mem)
        have h_aux' : sSup B ‚Č§ A.val := h_B‚āĀ ‚Ėł h_N_le_A
        have h_last : sSup B = (‚ä• : PersistenceSubmodule M) := by
          rw [disjoint_comm] at h_aux
          exact (Disjoint.eq_bot_of_le h_aux h_aux')
        rw [‚Üź h_B‚āĀ] at h_last
        subst h_last
        exact (J.not_bot_mem h_N_in_J)
      have h_A_le_N : A.val ‚Č§ N := by
        rw [h_B‚āĀ]
        exact le_sSup h_mem
      have h_A_eq_N : A.val = N := by
        exact (le_antisymm h_A_le_N h_N_le_A)
      have h_contra : N ‚ąą I := by
        exact h_A_eq_N ‚Ėł A.prop
      aesop
    have h_final_right : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą I, N ‚ąą J := by
      sorry
    aesop

section Indecomposable
variable {D : DirectSumDecomposition M}

/-- If `D` is a direct sum decomposition of `M` and for each `N` appearing in `D` we are given a
direct sum decomposition of `N`, we can construct a refinement of `D` whose underlying set is the
union of all decompositions of the `N`'s appearing in `D`. -/
@[nolint unusedArguments]
def refinement (B : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą D, Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))
    (hB : ‚ąÄ N hN, N = sSup (B N hN)) (hB' : ‚ąÄ N hN, sSupIndep (B N hN))
    (hB'' : ‚ąÄ N hN, ‚ä• ‚ąČ B N hN) : DirectSumDecomposition M where
  carrier := ‚čÉ N, ‚čÉ hN, B N hN
  sSupIndep' x hx a ha ha' := by
    sorry
  sSup_eq_top' := by
    sorry
  not_bot_mem' := by simp [Set.mem_iUnion, hB'']

/-- The direct sum decomposition `refinement B hB hB' hB''` is a refinement of `D` -/
lemma refinement_le (B : ‚ąÄ N ‚ąą D, Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))
    (hB : ‚ąÄ N hN, N = sSup (B N hN))
    (hB' : ‚ąÄ N hN, sSupIndep (B N hN))
    (hB'' : ‚ąÄ N hN, ‚ä• ‚ąČ B N hN) :
    D ‚Č§ refinement B hB hB' hB'' := sorry

/-- If one of the decompositions of the `N`'s is non-trivial then `refinement B hB hB' hB''`
is a strict refinement of `D` -/
